claim: Hand B Layer-1 inner inventory partitions into VOWEL-INNER class {i,e,o,a} and CONSONANT-INNER class {d,k,ch,t,ckh,r} with the same complementary Layer-2 outer selectional pattern as Hand A: every top-5 outer shows >=3x V/C differential, with at least one outer showing strict selectional restriction. Sub-test 1b of H-BV-HAND-B-FULL-BATTERY-01.
evidence_for: Hand A's H-BV-SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01 found MARGINAL ordering ratios (V 0.985, C 1.055) but a strong M2 outer-preference signal: every top-5 outer shows >=3x V/C differential, n is vowel-exclusive (0/2240 cons-inner attachments). The complementary outer-selection pattern is the substantive finding.
H-BV-HAND-B-SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01 (sub-test 1a, just executed) confirmed Hand B has the same 4-vowel / 6-consonant top-10 split, with same 4 vowels and structurally analogous consonant set.
Both hands share the manuscript-wide Markov-1-flat character signature. Sub-class selectional patterns are likely also shared if morphology is uniform.

Hand B's consonant set differs slightly from Hand A's (B has ckh, r where A has sh, l). This provides a natural test: if the V/C-selectional pattern holds despite different consonant glyphs, the partition is generalisable; if not, the pattern was Hand-A-specific.
evidence_against: Hand B's most-frequent outers (from sub-test 1a: y, n, r, l, ol) overlap but are not identical to Hand A's (y, n, r, ol, l). Selectional patterns may shift.
Hand A's M2 outer-preference table showed n vowel-exclusive (0/2240). If the same n-exclusivity does NOT hold in Hand B, the sub-class structure is hand-specific.
Hand B has section-coupled vocabulary (H-BV-HAND-B-MARKER-CONCENTRATION-01); section-specific markers might disrupt the V/C selectional pattern when computed on whole Hand B.

The qo-k- biological-amplification finding (H-BV-QO-K-MORPHOLOGICAL-CLASS-01) means k-inners are biological-concentrated. This could shift outer preferences if biological folios use distinctive outer patterns.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE — IDENTICAL METHODOLOGY TO H-BV-SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01 with Hand B substitutions.

  DATA: Hand B tokens under Tokenisation A (MULTI_GLYPHS + 'ol'), N>=3 glyph-units (matches H-BV-HAND-B-SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01).

  PARTITION (LOCKED, derived from Hand B top-10 inners measured in 1a):
    VOWEL-INNERS = {i, e, o, a}  (same 4 vowels as Hand A)
    CONSONANT-INNERS = {d, k, ch, t, ckh, r}  (Hand B specific — swap sh,l for ckh,r vs Hand A)
    TOP-5 OUTERS = {y, n, r, l, ol}  (from sub-test 1a)

  METRICS (locked, identical to Hand A SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01):

  M1 ORDERING RATIO PER SUB-CLASS:
    For S in {VOWEL, CONSONANT}:
      ratio_S = P(outer in TOP-5 | inner in S) / P(outer in TOP-5 | inner NOT in S)
      where 'NOT in S' = all non-S inners in Hand B N>=3 token pool.

  M1b COMBINED RATIO (sanity check):
    ratio_combined = P(outer in TOP-5 | inner in VOWEL ∪ CONSONANT) / P(outer in TOP-5 | inner outside top-10)
    Should reproduce ~ Hand B SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01 C4 ratio (1.14) for sanity.

  M2 SUB-CLASS OUTER PREFERENCE (the central finding for Hand A):
    For each top-5 outer o:
      p_VOWEL(o) = count(inner ∈ VOWEL-INNERS, outer = o) / count(inner ∈ VOWEL-INNERS)
      p_CONSONANT(o) = count(inner ∈ CONSONANT-INNERS, outer = o) / count(inner ∈ CONSONANT-INNERS)
      diff(o) = p_VOWEL(o) / p_CONSONANT(o)  (or 'vowel-exclusive' if p_C = 0; 'cons-exclusive' if p_V = 0)
    Report full 2x5 table.

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED, identical to Hand A):
    CONFIRMED: max(ratio_VOWEL, ratio_CONSONANT) >= 2.0
    REFUTED: ratio_VOWEL < 1.5 AND ratio_CONSONANT < 1.5 AND no outer o has diff(o) >= 3.0 or <= 1/3.0
    MARGINAL: any other combination

  PARALLEL COMPARISON: report side-by-side Hand A (from SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01) vs Hand B (this test). For Hand A: M1 ratios 0.985/1.055; M2 every outer >=3x differential; n vowel-exclusive (0/2240).
test_metric: ratio_VOWEL, ratio_CONSONANT, diff(o) for o in {y,n,r,l,ol}
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if max ratio >=2.0. REFUTED if both <1.5 and no outer >=3x. MARGINAL otherwise.

M2_outer_preference.note: 1.1x cons-preferring (FAILS 3x threshold; differs from Hand A where ol was 3.4x cons-preferring)

hand_a_comparison.n_vowel_exclusivity: REPLICATED — Hand A 0/2240, Hand B 0/7403 (both categorical)
hand_a_comparison.ol_difference: Hand A: ol cons-preferring at 3.4x; Hand B: ol near-symmetric at 1.1x. The ol selectional asymmetry is Hand-A-specific.

tests_run.interpretation: Strong replication with one specific divergence. The CORE finding — n is vowel-exclusive — reproduces categorically in Hand B (0/7403 cons-inner attachments matching Hand A's 0/2240). The complementary outer-selection pattern (n, r, l vowel-preferring; y consonant-preferring) holds for 4 of 5 outers. The ol outer is the differing case: Hand A had ol cons-preferring at 3.4x; Hand B has ol nearly symmetric (1.1x). The ol-cons-preferring pattern is therefore Hand A-specific. M1 ratios match Hand A's MARGINAL pattern (both hands ~1.0). Net: V/C sub-class selectional structure is manuscript-wide; one specific pairing (ol-consonant) is Hand A-specific.

reasoning: Sub-test 1b of the systematic Hand B replication. Locks Hand-B-specific V/C partition (same vowels, different consonants matching Hand B top-10) and runs identical M1+M2 methodology as SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01. The substantive question: does Hand A's complementary outer-selection pattern (n vowel-exclusive, every outer >=3x differential) reproduce in Hand B?
